key_insight: The paper introduces the Nanotechnology Molecular Optimization (NMO) Benchmark, replacing drug-discovery proxy objectives with physics-based quantum simulations to make molecular generative models solve scientifically grounded nanotechnology tasks.
engineering_relevance: NMO provides a more realistic testbed for materials discovery, helping engineers identify methods that transfer beyond pharmaceutical benchmarks and into quantum materials design.
method: Use quantum simulations as the objective oracle instead of proxy property predictors.
Impose strict structural constraints and rugged fitness landscapes to reflect real nanotechnology design challenges.
Add a domain-agnostic pretraining strategy plus a new representation for structural constraints to reduce pharmaceutical bias.
evidence_signal: Across the NMO tasks, advanced molecular optimization methods underperform simpler approaches, while the proposed baseline improves physical-property results and uncovers previously unknown structural motifs.
watch_item: Because the benchmark uses hard physics-based constraints and costly quantum evaluations, performance may be sensitive to representation quality and computational expense.
paper_title: Beyond Drug Discovery: The Nanotechnology Molecular Optimization (NMO) Benchmark
